description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a bulky, box-like form and a wood-textured exterior casing. The front features a convex glass screen with a slightly frosted appearance, surrounded by a dark bezel. To the right of the screen, there is a metallic control panel with several rotary dials and push buttons, typical of mid-20th-century TV designs. The sides and back are deep and angular, with a pronounced tapering toward the rear, indicating the presence of a large cathode ray tube inside. The top and sides are finished in a dark, woodgrain pattern, while the back is matte black and featureless. This television would likely be found discarded or as a relic in urban environments such as alleys, garages, or old apartment buildings.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a woodgrain exterior, convex glass screen, and a side control panel with dials and buttons.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, woodgrain bezel, and metallic control panel with dials and buttons.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the deep, angular profile and the woodgrain texture along the side.
description_view_2: Top view displaying the flat, woodgrain surface and the pronounced depth of the television's body.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the angular taper and woodgrain finish.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, dark brown woodgrain bezel with a metallic control panel on the right
attribute_control_button_layout: cluster of dials and buttons on the front right panel
